claim: Regression archives are useful because they convert failures into reusable boundaries for future chips.
mechanism: A contradiction that only lives in a local fix is easy to repeat elsewhere. When regressions are archived with the failed assumption, the observed effect, and the resolution path, future chips can inherit the lesson without re-paying the same debugging cost. That makes contradiction handling an ecosystem asset rather than a local maintenance chore.
boundary: Archives should focus on meaningful regressions with transfer value. Not every defect deserves durable promotion.
